src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Refactor SecurityFilterChain configuration to use the new Spring Security 6.x OAuth2AuthorizationServerConfigurer API, replacing the deprecated manual configurer application and matcher logic.
Remove the custom OidcUserInfo mapper bean and configuration, likely relying on default behavior or a different implementation strategy in the new version.
src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.human_impl: Removed Function<OidcUserInfoAuthenticationContext, OidcUserInfo> userInfoMapper and associated oidc.userInfoEndpoint configuration.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Replace the deprecated Ordered.HIGHEST_PRECEDENCE constant with a simple integer value for the @Order annotation.
Add @EnableWebSecurity annotation to the configuration class, which is required for Spring Security 6.x to properly initialize the security filter chain.
Define a new bean for RegisteredClientRepository using a custom implementation that likely integrates with the application's persistence layer and caching.
Define a new bean for ClientRegistrationRepository using a custom JdbcClientRegistrationRepository implementation.
Update import statements to reflect the new package structure and API changes in Spring Security 6.x, removing deprecated classes and adding new ones.
Replace manual SimpleCacheManager with Spring's dedicated CaffeineCacheManager to leverage built-in registration methods.
Explicitly disable null values in the cache manager to prevent potential runtime issues or unexpected behavior.
Add logic to handle cache entries with zero or negative TTL by omitting expiration settings.
Migrate Jackson imports from com.fasterxml.jackson to tools.jackson to align with Spring Boot 3.5+ dependency changes.
Update JsonGenerator creation to use the new API method createGenerator on ObjectMapper instead of getFactory().createGenerator.
Migrate Jackson imports from com.fasterxml.jackson to tools.jackson to align with Spring Boot 3.5+ dependency changes.
Update JsonGenerator creation to use the new API method available in the migrated Jackson version.
Replace string-based charset encoding with StandardCharsets.UTF_8 to remove checked exception handling.
Switch from string concatenation to placeholder-based logging for better performance and readability.
Capture and store client_id in login history for better audit trails.
Replace explicit cast with Java 16+ pattern matching for instanceof to simplify code.
Replace Apache Commons StringUtils.equalsIgnoreCase with Strings.CI.equals for cleaner API usage.
Remove manual null check for loadUserByUsername result, relying on exception handling instead.
Remove debug log statements for authentication failures to reduce noise.

Extract client_id from the saved request parameters to include it in the login history record.
Add client_id to the LoginHistory entity for better audit tracking.
Enrich login history remarks with AJAX status and redirect URL for debugging purposes.
Move isAjax calculation earlier in the method to allow its value to be used in the login history remarks.
Remove redundant logger check before throwing an exception, relying on the exception message for context.

Migrate CSRF configuration from the deprecated fluent API to the lambda-based DSL required in Spring Security 6.
Migrate logout configuration from the deprecated fluent API to the lambda-based DSL required in Spring Security 6.
Migrate form login configuration from the deprecated fluent API to the lambda-based DSL required in Spring Security 6.
Migrate exception handling configuration from the deprecated fluent API to the lambda-based DSL required in Spring Security 6.
Add OAuth2 Login configuration block to the security filter chain, likely to support new authentication flows in the updated Spring Boot version.
Explicitly define the security matcher for the filter chain to scope it to specific paths, replacing implicit global application.
Remove the explicit AuthenticationProvider bean definition, likely because it is now auto-configured or handled differently in the new Spring Security version.
Remove the explicit injection and usage of AuthenticationDetailsSource, as the new Spring Security version handles this internally or via different mechanisms.

src/main/java/com/revengemission/sso/oau.general_idea: Add @Order annotation to the SecurityFilterChain bean to ensure correct precedence when multiple filter chains are defined.
Replace the generic custom success handler with a specific FederatedIdentityAuthenticationSuccessHandler, possibly to handle OAuth2/OIDC flows correctly.
Migrate from a custom, manual captcha generation and verification logic to the 'tianai-captcha' library.
Refactor the controller to use Spring's `InitializingBean` for library initialization and standardize endpoint mappings.
